meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve 'No Project Ideas'
content.seo_description: Stop guessing. Learn how to use advanced analytics to identify market gaps, solve the 'no project ideas' dilemma, and build high-converting SaaS products.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' paralysis stems from guessing rather than observing. By instrumenting micro-SaaS prototypes with granular event tracking, you shift from ideation to data-driven discovery. Use Antigravity to scaffold your tracking layer and Claude Code to automate the deployment, turning raw user behavior into high-value product roadmaps.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the silent killer of the modern founder. You sit at your desk, armed with a powerful tech stack, yet you remain paralyzed by the existential dread of building something nobody wants. This isn't a lack of creativity; it is a lack of feedback loops. You are trying to invent demand instead of discovering it.

True product-market fit isn't found in a flash of inspiration. It is excavated from the data of user interactions. By deploying an advanced analytics layer early, you turn your MVP into a research laboratory. We are moving beyond vanity metrics to capture the 'intent signals' that reveal where your users are actually struggling.

steps.title: Define Intent-Based Tracking Events
steps.description: Don't track page views; track friction points. Map out the exact interactions that signal a user is solving a problem, such as 'export_failed' or 'search_no_results'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a tracking schema for a SaaS MVP that captures high-intent friction signals: search_query_null, feature_usage_time, and conversion_dropoff_path.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --file schema.json --goal 'Implement event tracking middleware based on schema'

steps.title: Inject Analytics Middleware
steps.description: Decouple your business logic from your tracking. Use middleware to ensure that every user action is captured without polluting your core codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript middleware function for Express that captures request payload and user metadata, then pushes it to a stream-based analytics endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --path ./middleware/analytics.ts --context 'Integrate with existing Express router'

steps.title: Implement 'No-Result' Capture
steps.description: This is the 'Golden Metric'. When a user searches for something your app doesn't provide, that is your next project idea.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an event handler that triggers when a search query returns zero results and logs the query string to a private database table.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor --goal 'Capture empty search results and log to database'

steps.title: Automate Data Visualization
steps.description: If you don't look at the data, it doesn't exist. Use Claude Code to build a simple internal dashboard that highlights your top 5 'friction queries'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a simple React component that fetches the top 5 'empty search' strings from the API and displays them as actionable insights.

steps.title: Iterative Deployment Loop
steps.description: Deploy your telemetry, gather 100 sessions, and use the data to validate your next pivot. Speed is your only advantage.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a CI/CD script that validates the analytics schema before deployment to ensure no data loss during updates.

pro_tips: Track 'rage clicks' as a proxy for UI/UX confusion, not just conversion events.
Always log the user's current context (URL, session state) alongside the failure event to reproduce the problem.
Use a low-cost, serverless database for your analytics storage to prevent scaling costs from killing your MVP.
If your 'no results' logs are empty, you don't have a product problem; you have a marketing and traffic problem.
Never ship an event that isn't connected to a specific business hypothesis you are trying to validate.
resources: PostHog Event Schema Best Practices: https://posthog.com/docs/data/events
content.next_steps: Once your dashboard shows 50+ 'no result' events, group them by theme. Build the simplest version of that feature, deploy it, and watch if the events shift from 'no results' to 'success'.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start engineering based on real user intent. Your next breakthrough is hiding in your analytics logs. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
